title: Memoize Expensive Computation
description:
Optimize a dashboard that recalculates expensive statistics on every render, even when the underlying data hasn't changed.

### Requirements

- Use `useMemo` to cache the expensive `computeStats` calculation
- Stats should only recompute when the `transactions` data changes, NOT when the theme toggles or search updates
- Track recomputation count to prove memoization works
- The theme toggle and search filter must still work correctly
- The filtered list should also be memoized

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Theme toggle | `theme-toggle` |
| Search input | `search` |
| Total stat | `stat-total` |
| Average stat | `stat-average` |
| Max stat | `stat-max` |
| Compute count | `compute-count` |
| Transaction row | `tx-{index}` |
constraints:
- Must use useMemo (not useEffect + state)
- computeStats must only run when transactions change
- Filtered list must be memoized separately from stats
- Theme toggle must not trigger recomputation
- The compute counter must be accurate
hints: useMemo takes a factory function and a dependency array. Put [transactions] as the dependency so it only recomputes when transactions change.
Use a ref to count computations — increment it inside the useMemo factory so it only increments on actual recomputation.
Memoize the filtered list with useMemo([search, transactions]) — separate from the stats memo.
